description:
Third subticket of fz-ul4.10. Depends on .10.1 (reification) and .10.2 (quote/unquote).

The actual lift that makes `defmacro` mean something. Between parse and type-check:

1. Walk every Item::Fn body. For each Expr::Call(name, args) where name is a defmacro:
   - Reify each arg as a Value via expr_to_value (do NOT eval the args — macros receive their args quoted, just like Elixir).
   - Run the macro body in the interp with a fresh frame binding the macro's params to those reified Values.
   - Decode the macro's return Value back to Expr via value_to_expr.
   - Splice the returned Expr in place of the original Call.
2. Re-walk the result (macros can expand to calls of other macros).
3. Cap recursion at some limit (say 100) to avoid runaway expansion.

After expansion, the typer + rest of the pipeline see only ordinary Exprs.

Tests: a defmacro that takes `x` and returns `quote do: unquote(x) + 1` — calling it with `m(41)` should yield 42 at runtime.
title: Macro expansion pass
